era: 1998-present. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Krautrock/Experimental. E=5. First Krautrock entry. Genre Stage 92. Sub Pop. Capitol Hill streak begins (110-115).

location.formative: Seattle's experimental and heavy music scene. Kinski channels krautrock's motorik rhythms and hypnotic repetition through Pacific Northwest volume and weight. Capitol Hill is the gravitational center.
location.career: Kinski -- krautrock/experimental band. 1998-present. Sub Pop. Extended, hypnotic, heavy instrumental jams. Motorik rhythms. Capitol Hill.

associated_musicians.name: No-Anchor
associated_musicians.note: Noise/Experimental (109, E=6) and Krautrock/Experimental (110, E=5). No-Anchor collapses; Kinski sustains. The energy correction: chaos to control.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Both heavy, both Seattle. Hovercraft drones (E=4); Kinski drives (E=5). Stasis vs. propulsion.

associated_musicians.name: Earth
associated_musicians.note: Both extremely heavy, both extended-form. Earth is glacial (E=3); Kinski is propulsive (E=5). Different velocities of heaviness.

associated_musicians.name: Mudhoney
associated_musicians.note: Both on Sub Pop. Mudhoney: garage/grunge (E=5). Kinski: krautrock/experimental (E=5). Same label, same energy, different decades and genres.

associated_musicians.name: Jimi Hendrix
associated_musicians.note: Both push electric guitar into experimental territory. Hendrix through rock (E=6); Kinski through krautrock (E=5). Hendrix explodes; Kinski hypnotizes.

theory_nodes.concept: The hypnotic engine (one hundred eleventh acoustic element -- motorik rhythmic repetition sustained long enough to induce trance-like state; layered instrumentation accumulates above the unchanging beat; temporal suspension; constructive where the collapse frequency was destructive; patient where the punk circuit was explosive)
examples: Kinski (degree 110) -- krautrock/experimental. Motorik beat + guitar accumulation. E=5. Hypnotic repetition.
Contrast with the collapse frequency (degree 109, No-Anchor) -- destructive. The hypnotic engine is constructive.
Contrast with the punk circuit (degree 108, Veronica Lipgloss) -- explosive. The hypnotic engine is patient.
Contrast with the glacial drone (degree 96, Earth) -- static. The hypnotic engine is kinetic and propulsive.
theory_nodes.description: The hypnotic engine is the 111th acoustic element. Trance-inducing motorik repetition with heavy instrumental accumulation.

ancestors: The collapse frequency (degree 109, No-Anchor) -- destructive. The hypnotic engine is constructive.
The glacial drone (degree 96, Earth) -- static. The hypnotic engine is kinetic.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred eleven acoustic elements across one hundred ten degrees. Track element 112 at degree 111.

theory_nodes.concept: The energy correction (one hundred eleventh artist-city pattern -- after three consecutive E=6 maximum energy the engine returns to E=5; the correction is gentle; E=5 is still high energy; controlled repetition replaces chaos; sustainable intensity restored)
examples: Degree 110: Kinski at E=5. First sub-E=6 since degree 106 (Knife Knights, E=4).
Degrees 107-108-109: E=6, E=6, E=6. Three consecutive maximum. Unprecedented.
Degree 110: E=5. The correction. Controlled, hypnotic, patient. Still powerful.
Sequence: Production (107, E=6) -> Combustion (108, E=6) -> Collapse (109, E=6) -> Hypnosis (110, E=5).
theory_nodes.description: The energy correction is the 111th artist-city pattern. E=6 three-peat corrects to E=5. Sustainable intensity.

ancestors: The maximum three-peat (degree 109) -- three consecutive E=6. The correction follows.
E=5 last seen at degree 106 (Knife Knights, E=4 -- actually lower). Kinski restores E=5.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred eleven artist-city patterns across one hundred ten degrees. Track pattern 112 at degree 111.

theory_nodes.concept: Krautrock/Experimental at Genre Stage 92 -- 92nd genre stage; first Krautrock entry; German roots absorbed into Seattle's experimental vocabulary; motorik rhythms; hypnotic repetition; distinguished from Noise and Drone Metal
examples: First Krautrock entry in 110 degrees. The genre has been influential in Seattle's experimental scene but was not previously represented.
Distinguished from Noise (91, Hovercraft), Drone Metal (96, Earth), Noise/Experimental (109, No-Anchor). Krautrock is rhythmic and propulsive.
Lineage: Neu! (motorik), Can (improvisation), Faust (industrial textures) -> Kinski (Seattle weight + krautrock structure).
theory_nodes.description: Krautrock/Experimental at Genre Stage 92. First Krautrock. German roots in Seattle vocabulary. Motorik + Seattle weight.

ancestors: Noise/Experimental (degree 109, No-Anchor, Genre Stage 91). Sequential stages.
Noise (degree 91, Hovercraft). Same experimental territory, different approaches.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 92 at degree 110. Track at 111.

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill streak begins (degrees 110-115) -- six consecutive Capitol Hill artists; Seattle's primary music neighborhood; density of venues, studios, rehearsal spaces, and musicians' apartments; gravitational center of the scene
examples: Degree 110: Kinski -- Capitol Hill. First of six consecutive.
Degrees 111-115: five more Capitol Hill artists (to be documented).
Capitol Hill has appeared before in the engine, but never as a six-degree streak.
Key venues: Neumos, Barboza, Chop Suey, Kremwerk, The Vera Project.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill streak begins at degree 110. Six consecutive Capitol Hill artists (110-115). Neighborhood documented in depth.

ancestors: Prior Capitol Hill entries scattered across the engine. Now concentrated in a streak.

cross_references.relationship: CONSECUTIVE EXPERIMENTAL. Noise/Experimental (109, E=6) and Krautrock/Experimental (110, E=5). No-Anchor collapses; Kinski sustains. The energy correction: chaos to control.
EXPERIMENTAL LINEAGE. Both heavy, both Seattle. Hovercraft drones (E=4); Kinski drives (E=5). Stasis vs. propulsion.
HEAVINESS SPECTRUM. Both extremely heavy, both extended-form. Earth is glacial (E=3); Kinski is propulsive (E=5). Different velocities of heaviness.
SUB POP CONNECTION. Both on Sub Pop. Mudhoney: garage/grunge (E=5). Kinski: krautrock/experimental (E=5). Same label, same energy, different genres.
GUITAR EXPERIMENTATION. Both push electric guitar into experimental territory. Hendrix through rock (E=6); Kinski through krautrock (E=5). Explosion vs. hypnosis.
